lines.text: Agents are good at generating workflows. The hard part isn't capability — it's governance. ClarityLoop decides when an action is clear, safe, and authorized enough to ship. The model proposes; deterministic code decides.

An ambiguous request. ClarityLoop scores operational entropy deterministically — the model never invents a number — and loops for evidence. The commit residual falls. Once the authority boundary is clear and the evidence is sufficient, the gate clears it to commit.

On thirty-six cases, one uniform scorer. A capability-only agent commits unsafe thirty-six percent of the time. ClarityLoop drives that to zero, at eighty-six percent completion — and the completion gain is provably the evidence loop.
Workflows improve. ClarityLoop promotes a new version only when replay proves it is strictly safer than the one in production. Safety-weighted regression gating.

And it composes. Harnesses raise capability; ClarityLoop drives attack-success to zero. On AgentDojo, the gate blocks injected tool calls before they execute. Wrap any agent — keep its capability, remove its unsafe commits. ClarityLoop decides what ships.
